title: oenothera suffulta subsp. nealleyi
intro: Oenothera suffulta subsp. nealleyi, also known as Nealey's Evening Primrose, is a perennial plant species native to the southern United States and Mexico. As a member of the Onagraceae family, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenes. Herbal preparations of Nealey's Evening Primrose include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits.
